def: s.m. Cleverness, dexterity, skill, ability, capability, fitness.
s.m. A doctor, physician.
s.f. The medical profession.
s.m. One of a gang of robbers, a 'dacoit,' robber, highwayman, freebooter, pirate ( = ḍakait; syn. baṭ-pāṛ).

s.f. Split pea (of mūṅg, arhar, and other kinds of pulse, much used for food); split pulse; pulse, lentils, vetches; — a black or dark spot (resembling dāl, in anything); focus (of rays of light); — a crust, scale, scab
subs: (-kī), A scab to form; — a focus (of light) to be formed
s.m. Poor diet, coarse fare; pot-luck; something, some small gain
s.f. Bread and dāl; food, sustenance, livelihood
(-kisī-kī), 'The dāl to become soft in boiling'; to have an advantage, to avail; to succeed; to keep in (with), to get on (with)
'(There is) something black (as a fly or the like) in the dālʼ; (there is) something wrong or suspicious (in the affair, &c.), something amiss (here)
s.m. Flour and dāl; food, livelihood
s.m. 'One who lives on pulse boiled thin and watery'; a feeble person; a baniyā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀸𑀮𑀻 (dālī), from Sanskrit दाल (dāla), itself from दलति (dalati, “he splits”); see दॄ (dṝ, “to tear, divide, split”). Cognate with Marathi डाळ (ḍāḷ).

def: s.f. A branch, bough; a basket (made of twigs, or split bamboos) used to throw up water from ponds, &c. for irrigation (cf. ḍallā); irrigation of land by throwing up water from a pond, &c.; — blade (of a sword, or knife, &c., to which the handle is not attached)
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Ripened on the bough or tree
adj. (f. -ī), 'Plucked from the branch'; fresh; fine, excellent
adj. Of one piece, without seam or joining.

r: dāl, da, dāl-ě-muhmala, ta-varg, d, abjad, da-kār
def: s.m. The letter or sound da.
s.f. Colocynth.
s.m. A peculiar kind of tenure in the Doāb.

ety: Ap. Prk. डालउं, Prk. दालओ; S. दार+कः
def: s.m. A large branch; — a litter, &c. (see ḍallā).

s.m. A small shoot, leaf (of a tree); wild rice; — a lump, mass, heap, quantity; thickness, bulk, volume; a company, party or body (of men, united together for a common object, — hence, sometimes used in Hindī as a sign of the plural, e.g. kashī-dal, 'farmers'); a large army, a host; — destruction
subs: (˚la+āḍh˚), s.m. Name of several plants; the aquatic plant Pistia stratioites; a species of jasmine, Jasminum pubescens; wild sesamum; the plant Messua ferrea (com. called nagesar); the tree Acacia sirissa
(˚la+am˚), s.m. The plants Vangueria spinosa and Artemisia, &c.
s.m. A mass of clouds; a large army; a large tent or pavilion
s.f. The fragrant plant Pandanus odoratissimus (the leaves of which surround the flower; syn. ketkī)
adj. Thick, substantial, solid; full, fleshy, pulpy
(S. dala+snasā), s.f. The fibre or vein of a leaf
s.m. A folded leaf or petal
s.m. lit. 'Having young shoots incased in sheaths'; a sort of jasmine, Jasminum pubescens
s.m. The destruction of an army, or of a body of people
s.m. lit. 'Leaf-shedding'; a kind of birch (the bark of which is used for wrapping woollens in, and for ḥuqqa-snakes, &c.; syn. bhoj-patr)
s.m. 'Fragrance in the leaf'; the plant Echites scholaris.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دِل (dil). First attested in c. 1265 as Old Hindi دل (dl) and later as Middle Hindi دل (dl).

def: s.m. A piece, bit; a large lump, a clod (syn. ḍhelā).
s.m. A large (wicker-work) basket (see ḍāl); — a present of fruit, sweetmeats, &c. (sent by a bridegroom to the bride, &c.).
adj. See s.v. dalat.
s.f. pl. (of dalīl), Indications; arguments, proofs, evidences.
s.f. = daleṅtī, q.v.

ety: Prk. - S. डलकं or डल्लकं; prob. S. दर+कं
def: s.m. A basket, a sling, a ḍūlī carried on men's shoulders by means of a stick and ropes (like the beam and strings of a balance); a present of fruit, &c. (such being usually sent in this manner).
s.f. Shaking, trembling, quaking; tottering; — a shock, a blow.
s.f. Glitter, &c. ( = dalak, q.v.).
s.f. Glitter, splendour.
v.n. To shake, tremble, totter
v.n. To glitter, &c. ( = dalaknā, q.v.).
v.n. To shine, glitter, sparkle.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.

s.m. Amorous gesture or behaviour (of a woman), coquettish boldness with feigned opposition; looking (amorously or haughtily) through half-shut eyes; looking with affected anger (as lovers); cinœdus libidine infamis; — boldness, presumptuousness (towards a person beloved, or in whose estimation one holds a high place).

ety: for A. دلالة, inf. n. of دلّ 'to direct'
def: s.f. Direction, guidance, guide, mark, sign, token, indication; expression; argument, demonstration, proof, evidence; — brokerage ( = dallālī)
subs: (-kī), To betoken, to point (to), to indicate, show; to express, denote, signify, allude (to), typify; to prove, demonstrate.

def: s.m. Milk coagulated by runnet and formed into a lump; new cheese.
s.m. See s.v. dal.
s.m. A hall
adj. & s.m. Breaking to pieces, tearing, tearing asunder, rending; dividing, crushing; — a tearer, &c.
v.t. = dalwānā, q.v.
v.t. = ḍalwānā, q.v.
s.m. Refuse, rubbish, filth; a heap of rubbish, &c., a dung-hill, manure-heap, night-soil.
adj. Broken-hearted, cut to the heart.
adj. & s.m. Broken, crushed, beaten, defeated, dispirited; feeble, weak; — a crushed or feeble bird, &c. (esp. a lāl, q.v., — such a bird is used to train another of the same species to fight).
s.f. = dār-ćīnī, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. व्हल्, redupl.; — cf. also A. دلدل daldal, 'to shake'
def: s.f. Marshy land, mire, mud, marsh, swamp, bog, quagmire, slough, quicksand.
adj. (f. -ī), Marshy, swampy, quaggy, boggy.
s.f. Shaking, quivering, trembling, tremor ( = thartharāhaṭ).
v.n. To shake, quake; to undulate ( = thartharānā).

ety: fr. daldal; ˚ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. = daldalā, q.v.
s.m. A screen or cover in front of a bed.
s.m. See s.v. dala, dal.
s.f. A hand-mill for grinding, bruising, or splitting (grain, pulse, &c.).

ety: dale, infl. perf. part. of dalnā+panj, 'five'
def: s.m. 'The fives obliterated,' old age (in a horse), the third or last stage (in the term of a horse's life).

ety: contrac. of dalne-hārā = Prk. दलणिअ+अडओ (with h inserted)
def: s.m. = dalwaiyā, q.v.; — a dealer in grain, pulse, &c.

ety: fem. of dalhārā; ˚an = in = Prk. इणी = S. इनी]
def: s.f. A woman who deals in grain, &c.; the wife of a grain-dealer.

ety: fem. of dalhārā; ˚an = in = Prk. इणी = S. इनी]
def: s.f. A woman who deals in grain, &c.; the wife of a grain-dealer.
s.f. A clod of clay or earth (cf. ḍhelā).
s.f. A branch, a small branch; a twig; — a basket (to hold flowers or fruit); a basket of fruit, &c.; a present of fruit, sweetmeats, &c. (brought on a tray; cf. ḍalakam or ḍallakam).
s.f. A lump (of sugar, meat, &c.), a piece, bit; a kind of sweetmeat; a kind of betel-nut.
s.m. Poverty, &c. ( = daridr, q.v.); — mess, rubbish, refuse.
s.m. = H دالدري दालिद्री dālidrī, adj. corr. of dāridr; and dāridrī, qq.v.
s.f. = daridratā, q.v.
s.m. = daridrī, q.v.
adj. = H دالدر दालिद्र dālidr, s.m. corr. of dāridr; and dāridrī, qq.v.
s.f. Any sort of pulse, &c. which may be split (as dāl, gram &c.); split pulse.
s.f. Indication, evidence, argument, proof, demonstration; a director, guide, indicator, discoverer
subs: To adduce evidence, proof, or argument
(-par), To argue (on, or about); to dispute, be argumentative
(-par), To be a proof (of), to testify (to).

def: corr. of dāridr, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Argumentative; — a good arguer.

ety: Prk. दालिमं; S. दाडिमं, and दालिमं
def: s.m. = dāṛim, q.v.
s.m. The pomegranate ( = ḍāṛim).
s.m. Name of a class of Tagās in Murādābād.
part. Burst, split, broken, torn, rent.
s.m. Half-ground or coarsely-ground grain, bruised grain or pulse, split pulse, coarse meal; — a thin kind of rice-milk.
s.m. The man who throws up water with the ḍāl or basket.

ety: dim. of ḍāl; and ḍalā; — iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. 1˚ = ḍālī, 'branch,' q.v.; 2˚ = ḍalī, q.v.; — 3˚ A small basket (see ḍalā, and ḍalwā)
subs: s.m. 'Brushing or emptying out of the (sowing) baskets'; conclusion of the sowing season; the season of relaxation and rejoicing after the sowing.

ety: arabicized form of the P. dala, dalak, rt. Zend dal = S. दल्
def: s.m. A kind of patched garment, worn by many devotees, reputed saints, and darweshes
subs: adj. Wearing the dalq, clothed in rags.

def: s.m. See s.v. dal.

ety: act. part. n. of دلّ 'to direct,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Indicative (of), pointing (to); significant, expressive (of); typical; — indicator, index; indication, guide ( = dalīl)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀸𑀮𑀻 (dālī), from Sanskrit दाल (dāla), itself from दलति (dalati, “he splits”); see दॄ (dṝ, “to tear, divide, split”). Cognate with Marathi डाळ (ḍāḷ).

def: s.m. One who rubs, or rubs and presses, the body in a hot bath; a barber.
adj. Of or relating to a barber, &c.
s.m. One who brings together the seller and the buyer, a broker, a salesman.
s.f. The business of a broker; — brokerage, commission of a broker.

ety: by redupl. of ḍal˚ = S. टल्
def: v.n. (dialec.) = ḍagmagānā, q.v.

ety: Prk. दलणअं; S. दलनीयं, rt. दल्
def: v.t. To grind coarsely, to bruise, to split (pulse); — dal-mārnā, v.t. To crush to death; to trample or crush under the feet
subs: v.t. To squeeze and crumple, to rumple; to thrash

ety: ḍāl˚ = Prk. डार(इ) or डारे(इ) = S. दारय(ति), caus. of rt. दृ
def: v.t. To throw, fling, cast, hurl, drop; to produce or have an abortion of; to shed, pour, inject; to vomit; to place or lay (before), to submit, to present; to lay (on), to put or throw (on, as a garment, &c.); to keep (as a mistress), to put aside; to push; to set; to shake; to scatter, to sow broadcast; to sow (discord, &c.); to thrust; to destroy; to protrude; to involve; to cause, occasion, produce, excite: (ḍālnā annexed to the base of another verb, is used to form intensives, e.g. toṛ-ḍālnā, 'To break to pieces'; de-ḍālnā, 'To give away'; khā-ḍālnā, 'To eat up the whole')
subs: (with acc.), To throw down and go, to leave behind; to throw away; to leave, abandon, desert
v.t. (intens.), To throw down or away, &c., see ḍālnā
v.t. To put away, lay aside; to detain

def: v.n. To be thrown or cast; to be placed, or put, or kept.

ety: for A. دلالة dallālat, fem. of dallāl
def: s.f. A go-between, a procuress.
s.m. 1˚ = dāl-wālā, q.v.s.v. dāl; — 2˚ corr. of dālān, q.v.

ety: S. डलकः (with व inserted); cf. ḍāl; ḍalā
def: s.m. A large basket (without a lid).
s.m. A bucket (syn. dol, ḍol), an urn; the sign Aquarius; — the hopper of a mill.
s.f. Price paid for grinding or bruising pulse, &c.
s.m. One who grinds or bruises gram, pulse, &c.; a corn grinder; — a seller of grain and pulse, &c.

ety: doub. caus. of dalnā, and = dal(nā)+āw+āw = Prk. आवे, redupl.; S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to be coarsely ground, to have or get (pulse, &c.) bruised or split.
v.t. To cause to be thrown or thrown down, to have or get thrown down, to have (a thing) put down, or put on (as a garment, &c.); to put on, to wear; to occasion, &c. (see ḍālnā).

s.m. Money; price, value, cost (syn. mol; qīmat); — a copper coin, or a measure of money-value, equal to one twenty-fifth of a paisā (at the present time: in the time of Akbar the dām is said to have been of the value of one fortieth of a rupee)
subs: s.m. A purchased share (of an estate or village, &c.)
To pay, or pay in, the price or value (of, -kā), — to take the price or value (of), to charge (for)
s.m. The whole amount, every fraction to the last farthing
(-ko), To pay (one) the price or value (of, -kā).
ex: دوردرشن انچے یا سستے داموں پر خریدو
dūrdarśan ū̃ce yā saste dāmõ par xarīdẽ
Buy TV at a high or cheap prices
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫 (damma, “coin”), from Sanskrit द्रम्म (dramma), from Ancient Greek δραχμή (drakhmḗ).

ety: Prk. दामं; S. दाम (base दामन्), rt. दा 'to bind'
def: s.m.f. A rope, cord, string; a fetter.
ex: دوردرشن انچے یا سستے داموں پر خریدو
dūrdarśan ū̃ce yā saste dāmõ par xarīdẽ
Buy TV at a high or cheap prices
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫 (damma, “coin”), from Sanskrit द्रम्म (dramma), from Ancient Greek δραχμή (drakhmḗ).

def: s.m. A net, a snare
subs: s.m. One who uses a net or snare; a fisherman; a fowler, &c.
s.f. The laying of nets; — a branch of revenue arising from fowlers, players, musicians, &c.
(-ko), To bring (one) into a snare, to ensnare, entrap.
ex: دوردرشن انچے یا سستے داموں پر خریدو
dūrdarśan ū̃ce yā saste dāmõ par xarīdẽ
Buy TV at a high or cheap prices
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫 (damma, “coin”), from Sanskrit द्रम्म (dramma), from Ancient Greek δραχμή (drakhmḗ).

ety: Pehl. dām; Zend dāman, rt. dā; S. धामन्, rt. धा
def: s.m. 'Creature'; animal, any quadruped that is not rapacious (opp. to dad)
subs: s.m. Quadrupeds in general, wild animals (both rapacious and not rapacious), wild beasts.
ex: دوردرشن انچے یا سستے داموں پر خریدو
dūrdarśan ū̃ce yā saste dāmõ par xarīdẽ
Buy TV at a high or cheap prices
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫 (damma, “coin”), from Sanskrit द्रम्म (dramma), from Ancient Greek δραχμή (drakhmḗ).

ety: v.n. fr. dam-īdan, rt. Zend dam = S. धम्˚
def: s.m. Breath, vital air, life; — a moment, an instant; — breath or blast (of a furnace or oven); a puff, whiff, pull, draw (of a ḥuqqa); a draught (of water); stewing or simmering over a slow fire; — spring, elasticity; vitality, energy, vigour, stamina, spirit, mettle; strength, goodness, virtue (as of medicines, cloth, &c.); — ambition; — cheerfulness, pleasure, recreation; society (as a breathing together); — edge (of a sword); point (of a spear)
subs: adj. & adv. Continual; — continually, &c. ( = dam-ba-dam, q.v.)
v.n. To gasp or draw in the breath (as one who has had a severe fall, or is dying); to be suffocated or choked; to breathe one's last; — to be confounded, be confused
To be breathless in attention, to be very attentive
adj. Struck dumb, confounded, aghast; silent
adv. 'Breath by breath,' every moment, continually, constantly, perpetually, incessantly, successively, repeatedly
To make (oneself) long-winded; to practise holding the breath
v.n. To hold the breath; to be silenced, be unable to answer or say anything
adv. In a moment, in an instant

v.n. To speak, say a word, to say bo to a goose; to stir; — to speak (of, -kā), to sing the praises (of), to laud; to boast (of); to believe (in, -kā), to profess; — dam bharnā or bhar-jānā, v.n. To be, or get, out of breath, to be exhausted
s.m. Simmering, cooking in steam; a stew or soup cooked in a pot the mouth of which is fast closed with paste, &c., in order to keep in the essence of the meat
v.n. To breathe short, to become breathless
v.n. To be in the death agony
v.n. To be unable to hold one's breath; to be out of breath; to be dying
To hold the breath, to feign fainting or death; — to skulk (from, -se), to shirk ( = jī ćhipānā, q.v.)
v.n. To be out of breath, to heave, to pant
s.m. A kind of firework that burns at intervals
To breathe out or forth, to breathe one's last, to expire
s.m. Edge or temper (of a blade); — stamina, vigour
adj. Long-winded; continuing long; long-lived; elastic; — sharp-edged, well-tempered
To give up one's life, to die; to die (for, -par, -pe), be madly in love (with); to impart temper (to, -ko, steel), to temper (steel); — to let (a thing in a pot) simmer or stew over a slow fire
v.n. To be suffocated; to breathe short or with difficulty
To hold one's peace, to be content with a little
s.m. Keeping silence; hesitating; pretending or making a claim
v.n. To stop or hold the breath (as a religious exercise, as some faqīrs do for hours together; or feigning death; or in diving, swimming, &c.)
adj. & s.m.f. Agreeing (with, -se), concordant, consenting, harmonious, intimate, familiar; singing or reciting together; — a friend, an intimate, companion, consort; (in Mus.) an accompaniment
s.m. A cold sigh, a sigh of despair
s.m. A short sword; — a pistol
s.m. Breath and moving power, life and motion; existence; health and strength
(-par), To blow (as a fire); to blow, breathe, pronounce, repeat (an incantation, on or over, as a conjurer)
adj. Drawing in the breath; silent; breathing; gasping
s.f. Drawing in the breath; silence
v.n. To hold one's breath and be still; to remain silent; to be confounded or perplexed

(-kā), To persecute, worry, annoy, tease; — dam khānā, or khā-jānā, or khā-rahnā, v.n. To remain silent, be silent; be crest-fallen; to bear patiently, to be patient; to be dressed over a slow fire (a stew, &c.)
'To draw in the breath,' to remain quiet or silent, hold one's peace; — to take a pull (at the ḥuqqa), to have a smoke
adv. In a moment, in a twinkling
v.n. To be suffocated, be choked
(-kā), To have a pull (at the ḥuqqa), to smoke (the ḥuqqa)
(-kā), To be desirous (of); — to be affected by the fumes (of); — to have a smoke
To utter a word, breathe a syllable, to speak; to boast (of, -kā), &c. ( = dam bharnā, q.v.)
(-ke, or mere), To recover one's breath, to revive; to recover from a faint, &c.), to recover one's senses; to regain calmness or composure, &c.
Life to remain, to draw breath, to breathe, to exist
adj. & adv. Ready (as dinner, &c.); single, sole; alone, by oneself
or nāk-meṅ dam ānā or ā-jānā, To be greatly distressed; to be tired or worried to death; to be at the last gasp, to gasp for life
v.n. Breath to leave (the body), to breathe one's last, to expire; to die (for, -par), be deeply in love (with)
v.n. To be stewed or simmered over a slow fire, to be left on the fire after cooking (a stew, &c.).

def: s.m. Deceit, fraud, trickery, trick, coaxing, wheedling; — arrogance, pride, haughtiness; boasting
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, artful, treacherous; — an artful person, a deceiver, coaxer, wheedler
s.f. Deceiving, deception, trickery, imposture, artfulness, coaxing, wheedling
s.m. False hope, soft words; coaxing. soothing, comfort, encouragement
(-ko), To wheedle, coax, cajole, deceive, inveigle
(-ko), To put or turn off with a joke, &c.
(-ke), To be taken in, be deceived, be cajoled (by)
(-ko), To deceive, delude, wheedle, entice; to entrap, bring into one's meshes.

def: s.m. Blood
subs: s.m. The red, resinous, inspissated juice called dragon's blood, Sanguis draconis.

def: s.m. A rope, &c. ( = dām, q.v.); — a garland.
s.m. Taming, subduing; self-command, self-restraint, self-control, subduing the passions, temperance; endurance of painful austerities
subs: dam-ghokh, s.m. Name of a prince of the lunar race, king of Ćedī, father of Śiśu-pāl.

ety: fr. dam, q.v.; and = S. धम or धमक
def: s.m. A pair of bellows; — asthma.

r: dāma, dāma daulatu-hu, dāma z̤illu-hu, z̤illu-hum, dāma mulku-hu
def: (3rd. pers. s. of perf. of دوم 'to continue,' &c., used optat.), May (it) be perpetuated, or may (it) last, &c.
subs: May his fortune or prosperity continue
(or z̤illu-hum), May his (or their) prosperity continue
ex: دوردرشن انچے یا سستے داموں پر خریدو
dūrdarśan ū̃ce yā saste dāmõ par xarīdẽ
Buy TV at a high or cheap prices
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀫𑁆𑀫 (damma, “coin”), from Sanskrit द्रम्म (dramma), from Ancient Greek δραχμή (drakhmḗ).

def: s.m. A son-in-law; a bridegroom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَامَاد (dāmād). Doublet of جَن٘وَائِی (jãvāī). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi داماد (damad /⁠dāmād⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of dāmād, q.v.
s.f. State, or relationship, or right, of a son-in-law.
adj. & s.m. = ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v.
adj. corr. of ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v.
s.f. Rate or amount of assessment.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Taming, subduing; — tamer, subduer; one who subdues his passions; a hero ( = daman).
s.f. Heat, hot blast; ardour; glow; brilliance, glitter, sheen; flush, bloom.
s.m. = damakṛā, damarkā, q.v.

ety: damak˚, prob. = Prk. दवक्के (इ), fr. S. दव+कृ
def: v.n. To give forth heat; to glow, shine, glitter, glisten, flare; to bloom.
s.m. = damarkā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a country, the modern district of Tamluk.

ety: prob. fr. rt. Zend dam = S. dham˚; cf. damdama
def: s.m. A large kettle-drum; — noise; pomp, &c. ( = damdama).

ety: prob. fr. rt. Zend dam = S. dham˚; cf. damdama
def: s.m. A large kettle-drum; — noise; pomp, &c. ( = damdama).
s.m. = dāman, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Taming, subduing, overpowering; subduing the passions; passionless; — a hero, one who subdues his passions; — the flower Artemisia indica ( = donā).
( = S. दमयन्ती), prop. n. The bride of Nal (whose adventures form an episode in the great Sanskrit epic poem Mahābhārata); a lover.

ety: = S. दामन् 'a girdle,' &c., rt. dā, 'to bind'
def: s.m. Skirt (of a garment), petticoat; sheet (of a sail); foot, or declivity (of a mountain)
subs: adj. Spreading or expanding the skirts (of the robe); walking proudly, or gracefully

(-kā), To seize or to cling to the skirt (of); to come under the protection (of), to take refuge; to become an adherent or follower (of); — to surrender at discretion, to cry for mercy (from); — to detain (one) by holding the skirt (of his garment), to stop; to prevent; to oppose; — to be the accuser (of), seek redress (from), to be or become a claimant or plaintiff
'To spread out the skirt' (of one's garment); to beg, supplicate, petition; to desire
v.t. To cover, screen, shield, protect; to conceal or hide (a fault), to commit crimes secretly
v.n. 'To shake the skirt on rising'; to go away without taking anything (from a house); to rise quickly in displeasure, to shake off all connection (with a person or place)
(-se), 'To snatch the skirt (from)'; to refuse decidedly or rudely, to decline clownishly
(-se), To get (one's) skirt away (from), to shake off, get rid (of), escape, or get away (from)
(-ke), To cling, or hang on to the skirt (of); to come under the protection (of), to depend (upon), claim protection (from)
adj. 'Dragging or trailing the skirt'; walking with dignity and grace; — turning away (from), shunning, abandoning

adj. & s.m. Seizing the skirts (of), depending (on), attached (to); demanding justice, or seeking redress (from); — an adherent, a dependant; an accuser, prosecutor, plaintiff, claimant; — dāman-gīr honā (-kā), To seize the skirts (of), to attach (oneself or itself, to), cling (to), depend (on); — to seek justice or redress (from), to accuse, to prosecute; to persecute, to pursue
s.f. Attachment; dependence; friendship; — applying for justice, accusation, arraignment
s.m. The hem or embroidery of a skirt.

def: adj. Powerful; terrible; impetuous; fierce.
s.m. = dāman, q.v.

ety: dam, 'temper,' q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be tempered; to acquire elasticity; — v.t. To test the elasticity (of steel, or a sword) by bending (it).
s.m. A carbine; a blunderbuss.
s.m. The flower Artemisia indica ( = daman); — name of a jackal (com. called Damnā, e.g. kalīlā-o-damnā); — name of two kinds of metre.
s.f. A heroine, &c. (see daman).

s.f. Part of a woman's dress, a veil, a mantle; — a scrap of a shroud kept by the relations of a deceased person; a piece of cloth, inscribed with the genealogy of the priest of a deceased woman, which is placed on her shroud; — a saddle-cloth, furniture, housings; — an ornament worn on the forehead.
s.f. A number of ropes or strings (for tying cattle); a rope, &c. ( = dām, q.v.).
adj. Relating to the artemisia flower (see donā).
adj. To be subdued or restrained, tameable, subduable, restrainable.
s.m.f. A destroyer.
s.m. Dammar, resin (esp., in commerce, the resin of the sāl tree); pitch; a torch; — an object causing surprise.
s.m. = ḍamrin, ḍamru, q.v.
s.m. = ḍamru.
s.m. The whirl of a spindle (a spherical piece of leather or wood) on which the thread is wound ( = damakṛā, damkhaṛā).
s.m. A tabor or small drum shaped like an hour-glass (held in one hand and beaten with the fingers: it is one of the ten attributes of Śiva); anything shaped like an hour-glass; the knee-joint(?)
prop. n. Name of a merchant, who dying insolvent, his effects were divided among his creditors in proportion to their claims.
s.f. An equitable partition of the effects of an insolvent among his creditors; dividend (of a bankrupt's property); proportioning, equal assessment; composition.
adj. Of or relating to the blood; bloody, tinged with blood; plethoric, sanguineous.
s.f. (lit. 'Subduing men'), Name of the beautiful daughter of Bhīma, king of Vidarbha, and wife of Nala (whose story forms the subject of a celebrated episode of the epic poem Mahābhārata).
s.m. Deceit, deception, fraud, trickery; feigning, hypocrisy; wickedness; pride, haughtiness, arrogance; boast, self-inflation; ostentation
subs: s.m. One who uses boastful language, a boaster.

def: adj. & s.m. Acting deceitfully, deceitful, feigning, hypocritical; proud, arrogant; — a deceiver, an impostor, a hypocrite, &c.
adj. & s.m. Deceitful; hypocritical; — a deceitful person, a cheat; — a hypocrite; — a kind of crane, Ardea nivea.
s.m. A platform in a field on which a person is posted to protect crops.
s.m. = ḍauṅjā, q.v.

ety: prob. Zend dam, redupl. = S. धमधम, fr. धम rt. ध्मा
def: s.m. Sound of a drum; report or booming of cannon, and the like; — report, noise, tumult, din, clamour, stir, ado, pomp, show; — a kettle-drum, a drum ( = damāma); — a mound, a raised battery; name of a place near Calcutta which is an artillery station (anglice, ḍamḍam); — a cavalier; — fraud, deceit; fascination (see dam = S. dambha)

ety: damdamā˚ = Prk. दमदमा(इ) = S. दमदमाय(ति), fr. दम
def: v.t. To cause to move or vibrate, to shake.
s.f. An assessment
subs: s.f. Gross assets of a village.

ety: dām, q.v.+ī = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: s.m. A setter of snares, a fowler, a hunter, sportsman.
s.f. A kind of ḥuqqa, or pipe (for smoking).

ety: perf. part. of damīdan, rt. Zend dam = S. धम˚, rt. ध्मा
def: part. Blown, blossomed, shot forth, opened out, expanded, vegetated (a plant); blowing, blossoming; sprouting; broken forth (as the dawn of day).
s.f. Blowing (as of wind); blowing (of a flower), blossoming; sprouting.
s.f. A heroine, &c. (see damak).
s.f. Lightning.
s.m. (local) A hillock, an eminence.
s.m. A squirt; a pump; a fire-engine; — a jack or crane (for raising weights).
s.m. A squirt; a pump; a fire-engine; — a jack or crane (for raising weights).

s.m. (lit. 'Let it not breathe'), A religious ceremony very popular with the agricultural and lower classes in Upper Hindūstān, which consists in jumping into a fire and treading it out with the exclamation of dam-madār! dam-madār! (cf. dhammāl).
v.n. To glow, to sparkle, glitter, flash; — to be tempered (as steel); to bend with elasticity, to spring (in these senses, prob. formed from dam, q.v.).

r: dāmodar, dāma, udara, Kr̤ishṇa, lit, Yaśodā
def: (dāma+udara), prop. n. Name of Kr̤ishṇa; a proper name; (lit. 'Having a rope round the belly'; Yaśodā, the foster-mother of Kr̤ishṇa, having in vain passed a rope round his body, whilst a child, to keep him in confinement).
s.m. (local) The tree Xanthochynus pictorius or Stalagmites pictoria (called also de-phūl).
s.m. Wife and husband, master and mistress, the couple.
s.m. Master or lord of the house; master, lord.
s.m. Gold, silver, money, wealth, riches
subs: v.t. To sell off, to sell cheap.

def: s.m. The whirl of a spindle (a spherical piece of leather or wood) on which the thread is wound ( = damakṛā, damkhaṛā).
s.f. = damṛī, q.v.
s.f. One-fourth or one-eighth of a paisā (about three dāms); a nominal coin; — (fig.) a farthing, a sou; — a subdivision of land-measure (one damṛī = 25 kaććā bīghās).
s.m. Pain in the knee-joints.
s.m. Name of a low tribe of Rājpūts in the Benares district.
adj. To be tamed, tameable, to be subjected or controlled; tamed, subjected; — s.m. A young bullock, a steer that has to be tamed.

ety: Pehl. dān; Zend dāna; S. धान
def: An affix denoting the receptacle, place, or stand (for a thing; — e.g. pīk-dān; qalām-dān; shamʻ-dān, &c., qq.v.).

ety: fr. dānistan; Pehl. dāneśtan; rt. Zend dā, 'to know'
def: part. Knowing, understanding (used as last member of comp., e.g. qadr-dān, nukta-dān, &c., qq.v.).
corr. of dāṅw; and of dāṅt, qq.v.
s.m. = dāna, q.v.

ety: Pehl. dānek; Zend dāna; S. dhānā
def: s.m. Grain, corn; food; — a grain; seed, berry; chick-pea, 'gram'; (local) poppy-seed; — a speck; a bead; a pimple, pustule; — dice
subs: To cause a pigeon to bring up the grain in its crop (an act had recourse to to make a pigeon light for flying)
v.n. To caress by joining bills (as pigeons do), to bill
s.m. Billing, caressing (as pigeons do) by joining bills
s.f. A cursory or rough survey; a partial measurement or weighment (of the produce of a field); an assessment of the revenue upon a valuation of the standing crops
v.t. To feed (its young, a bird)
s.m. Meat and drink, provisions, food, victuals
(-meṅ), To put seed (into the ground), to sow; — to bait (a hook)
vulg. dāne-dār, adj. Granulated; having the appearance of being granulated; — containing grain; — apportioning jamʻ or any other contribution according to the actual produce
v.t. To mingle, confound, to destroy
(-ke āge or sāmhne), dāna denā (-ko), To throw grain (before), give grain (to), to feed (fowls, &c.)
s.m. One who clutches at, or who grudges, even a single grain; a miser, an avaricious person
s.m. A kind of embroidered neck-cloth (worn in cold weather over the cloak).

def: s.m. Giving, presenting, offering; giving in marriage; gift, present, donation, grant; liberality, charity, alms; anything demanded by law or by custom (as toll, &c.); — a religious rite in which the Brāhmans pronounce a charm or incantation over anything in the wish of a happy futurity and give it (dān) as a present (to another person); the thing so charmed and given away; — a dowry; — the fluid that flows from the temples of an elephant in rut
subs: s.m. 'A master of liberality,' an exceedingly liberal or munificent man
s.m. A deed of conveyance, a deed of gift by which land is conveyed to Brāhmans; — a will; — one to whom a grant may be made, or to whom by law property may be conveyed
s.m. A grantee, of Brāhman caste, to whom lands have been assigned by deed
s.f. A religious gift to a mendicant, or to a Brāhman priest
s.m. Almsgiving, charitable acts, charity; the rules or practice of almsgiving
adj. Liberally disposed, liberal, generous, charitable, munificent, bountiful
s.f. Beneficence of disposition, liberality, charitableness, &c.
v.t. To give away, to bestow alms, to give in alms; — v.n. To practise liberality, &c.
s.m. 'A hero in liberality,' an exceedingly liberal man
adj. Worthy of a gift, meriting a donation.

def: adj. & s.m. Wise, learned; — a wise man, a sage (pl. dānāyān)
subs: The learned of the age or time

def: s.m. corr. of dānav, q.v.

ety: for A. دناَءة, inf. n. of دنأ 'to be low, or ignoble,' &c.
def: s.f. Lowness, meanness, baseness.
s.f. Report, reverberation, booming (of cannon, &c.).
s.m. See s.v. dān, 'gift.'
s.f. Wisdom, knowledge.
s.f. The arabicized form of the next, q.v.
part. adj. & s.m. Knowing, intelligent; skilled; — one (or he) who knows ( = jānne-wālā).
s.f. Giving, bountifulness, liberality ( = dānpan).
s.m. A class of demons; a demon; a giant, a Titan; — adj. Belonging, or peculiar, to the Dānavas.
incorr. for danāʼat, q.v.
s.m. Oppression, injustice; injury; — punishment, penalty; fine mulct; — an oar; (see ḍāṅḍ).
s.m. The beam of a weaver's loom.

s.m. Stick, staff, rod; mace; stem, handle; a pole or measure (of four cubits); a measure of time (twenty-four minutes); — assault, attack, violence; correction, corporal chastisement, punishment; amercement, fine, penalty; — danḍāśram (˚ḍa+āś˚), s.m. The order of the staff, condition of a pilgrim
subs: s.m. 'Stick - assault,' actual violence, assault and battery
s.m. A small kind of fish, Cyprinus barbiger (?)

s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed)
s.f. A kind of kettle-drum, on which the hours are struck
adj. & s.m. Awarding or inflicting punishment; — one who administers correction or punishment; an avenger
s.f. The rule of inflicting punishment, the system of civil and military administration taught by Ćāṇakya and others

danḍ-vat, s.f. lit. 'Like a stick'; falling or lying prostrate, prostration, a mode of salutation among Hindūs, obeisance, bow (the complete prostration is now rarely practised, except before an idol, or a person of high caste or position)
To fall or lie prostrate, to do obeisance
adj. Worthy or deserving of punishment, guilty.

def: s.m. A stick, staff, cudgel, pole; a measure of length, a pole of four to six cubits; a particular measure of time, about twenty-four minutes; — an oar; the back-bone; a line; — punishment, amercement, fine, forfeit; retaliation, revenge; — high land (not culturable for lack of water; hence), sterile land
subs: To pay a fine, undergo a penalty or punishment, to suffer
To ply an oar, to row
(-se), To take a fine (from), to fine, amerce, punish; to take revenge, to retaliate

def: s.m. A stick, cudgel, rod; — the part of the arm above the elbow; — an athletic or gymnastic exercise (placing the hands on the ground and then bending down so as almost to touch the earth with the breast); — punishment, fine, amercement, mulct, compensation; retaliation; tax
subs: (-kā), To pay a fine, to make compensation, pay damages (for)
s.m. One who exercises himself at the ḍanḍ
To practise the gymnastic exercise of ḍand
(-par), To levy or impose a fine, or tax
adv. With the power of the arm and head, with all one's might
adj. Deserving of punishment, punishable, penal.

def: s.m. A tooth; a rib; a toothed instrument (used by weavers); anything styptic or astringent (that contracts the mouth); name of a purgative seed (prob. croton; cf. dantī); name of a certain herb; — a class of beggars whose practice it is to cut and wound their bodies if their applications are not immediately attended to; — a thief; — adj. Poor, needy, in want of food; foolish, ignorant, stupid, silly; bold, fearless, wilful; unconscionable; irreligious.
s.m. A landmark, a boundary line
subs: s.m. Frontier or boundary (between the lands of two proprietors, &c.).

def: s.m. Stick, club; pole (of a carriage), post, stake, &c. ( = ḍanḍā, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Fighting with sticks or staves, mutual cudgelling; single-stick.

def: s.m. Boundary line, field-boundary, landmark; raised ground, a ridge (of earth); a path, a road
subs: s.m. Frontier-boundary, or a boundary road, between two estates; — bar (of a harbour); — alliance, friendship.

def: s.m. A staff, club, bat, ensign-staff, pole, bar, beam (of a pair of scales); — the upper arm ( = ḍanḍ); — a wall enclosing a house; — a collector of market-dues ( = ḍanḍiyā)
subs: v.t. To lift one up by the arms and legs (representing a ḍolī with its pole)

s.f. A mode of torture (it consists in fastening a man's arms behind his back with a cord, which is twisted round by means of a stick so as to increase the tension until the pain inflicted becomes intolerable)
adj. (f. -ī), Like a stick; cylindrical; (in Bot.) terete

def: s.m. A rib; — the middle of a mountain; — adj. Lost.

s.m. Stick, staff, pole, &c. (see danḍ); — a species of metre (the stanza of which exceeds twenty-seven, and may extend to two hundred syllables); — name of a district in the Dakkhan, between the Narbadā and Godāvarī rivers (which, in the time of Rāmćandra, was a forest, and celebrated as a place of pilgrimage); — the inhabitants of this district
subs: (˚ka+ar˚), and danḍak-van, s.m. The Danḍaka forest (in which for some time Rāma resided; — see above).

def: s.f. The country of Danḍaka (see above).
s.m. A tooth
subs: To make a crushing reply or rejoinder, to silence (a person)
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat, barley-sugar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَنْدَان (dandān). Doublet of دانت (dā̃t).

def: s.m. Tooth (of a saw, &c.); anything resembling a tooth; a notch, a jag
subs: (-meṅ), Notches or jags to form (in, a knife, &c.), to become notched or jagged.

def: v.n. To sing for joy, be in high spirits; to enjoy oneself, to be contented and independent, to live at ease.
adj. Punishable, to be chastised, deserving punishment, liable to a fine.
s.m. A blister; a pimple.
corr. of ḍanḍwat, q.v.
adj. Standing erect.

ety: Prk. दंडिओ; S. दण्डिकः (˚इन्+कः); and Prk. दंडिओ; S. दण्ड+इका
def: s.m. A rower: a waterman ( = ḍānḍī); — s.f. A balance.
s.m. (f. -inī), A staff-bearer; a religious mendicant who carries a staff in his hand; an epithet of Yama; a mace-bearer ( = danḍin).
s.f. (local) = ḍanḍī (the more com. form, q.v.).
s.m. An oarsman, a rower.
s.f. The beam of a pair of scales; — a carpet swung on a pole and carried on the shoulders of two men (used for the conveyance of residents in the hills, and called in English 'a dandy').
s.m. A religious mendicant carrying a staff; anyone carrying a staff; — a weighman.

s.f. Beam (of a pair of scales); — pole or shaft (of a vehicle); a lever; a handle; stalk, stem, &c. ( = ḍanṭhā, ḍaṅṭhī, q.v.), the tube of the corolla of the Nyctanthes arbortristis (used for dyeing); — the penis, yard; — an ear-ring affixed to the border of the ear; — a foot-path, path; — a line, stripe, &c. (see ḍanḍīr)
subs: s.m. Holder of the beam (of scales), weighman
(-kī), To press down the weight arm of a balance by a twist of the finger, to give short weight.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Inflicting punishment, punishing, chastising; — s.m. Chastiser, punisher.
s.m. One who carries a stick or staff; a mace-bearer ( = ćob-dār), &c. (see danḍī).

ety: ḍanḍī, 'line'+pleon. suff. īr = Prk. इल्ली = S. इली
def: s.f. Line, stripe, trace, score, lineament.
adj. Striped, marked with lines.
part. Punished, chastised, amerced, sentenced, mulcted, fined.
s.m. (lit. 'striped') A kind of garment for women, a mantilla; — a collector of market duties.
v.t. To punish, chastise, inflict a penalty on, to mulct, fine.

ety: ḍāṅḍ˚ = Prk. दंड़(इ) or दंड़े(इ = S. दण्डय(ति), rt. दण्ड्
def: v.t. To punish, fine, amerce; to pay off, take revenge on, retaliate on (e.g. ulṭe ćor koṭwāle ḍāṅḍe).
v.t. = ḍāṅḍnā, q.v.; — (fr. the trans. ḍāṅnā), v.n. To suffer punishment; to be fined, be taxed.
s.m. A south wind.
s.f. See s.v. danḍ.
s.f. Prostration (before a god or a superior), obeisance, Hindū salutation, bow
subs: To prostrate oneself, to do obeisance.

def: adj. = danḍanīya, q.v.
s.f. A small denomination of money, the sixth part of a dīnār, q.v.; — a weight, the fourth part of a drachm; — a sixth part (of anything); side or quarter (of a town); a side.
s.f. A hill, a precipice; summit (of a mountain); high bank (of a river; also written ḍāṅg).
adj. Struck, astonished, amazed, confounded, demented, stupefied, stupid, foolish; careless
subs: dang honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be astonished, &c.
s.m. Magnificence, grandeur, pomp (such as amazes or confounds).

def: s.f. A stick, club; — ridge or summit of a hill or mountain; hilly and wild tract of country, high land (see ḍāṅḍ).
s.m. Wrangling, row, fray, confusion, hubbub, disturbance, tumult, riot, rebellion, mutiny, sedition
subs: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, pugnacious, turbulent, riotous, seditious, rebellious, mutinous; — a turbulent fellow; one who incites to rebellion
To create a disturbance or hubbub, &c., to commit a riot, to incite to rebellion.

ety: daṅgā+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः); — and ˚ait = Prk. आइत्तो, or आयंतो, or आवेंतो = S. आपयन् the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, pugnacious, turbulent; refractory, seditious, rebellious, mutinous; — a quarrelsome, or turbulent fellow, a brawler; one who creates a disturbance, or who incites to rebellion, a seditious person, a mutineer, &c. ( = dange-bāz).

ety: daṅgā+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः); — and ˚ait = Prk. आइत्तो, or आयंतो, or आवेंतो = S. आपयन् the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, pugnacious, turbulent; refractory, seditious, rebellious, mutinous; — a quarrelsome, or turbulent fellow, a brawler; one who creates a disturbance, or who incites to rebellion, a seditious person, a mutineer, &c. ( = dange-bāz).
s.m. A kind of chair.
s.m. A tumultuous assembly, a crowd; — the sitting face to face in an assembly; — an amphitheatre; arena (esp. for wrestling)
subs: dangal jamnā, An amphitheatre to be filled, an assembly to be gathered in an amphitheatre

def: s.m. Horned cattle ( = ḍāṅgar); — a term of abuse, ox, fool, idiot.

ety: ḍāṅg, q.v.+Prk. अडो and अडओ; S. अ+र and र+कः
def: adj. Lean, thin, half-starved; — s.m. A starveling; a lean beast; a beast, horned cattle; — blossoming or flowering stem of radishes or mustard; — (dialec.) the plant producing the dark green pumpkin, and its fruit (cf. S. ḍaṅgarī, and ḍāṅgarī).
s.m. Name of a clan of Rājpūts in Ghāzipur.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to hilly country: — a name given to Bundelā Rāipūts.

ety: ḍāṅg, q.v.+Prk. अडो and अडओ; S. अ+र and र+कः
def: adj. Lean, thin, half-starved; — s.m. A starveling; a lean beast; a beast, horned cattle; — blossoming or flowering stem of radishes or mustard; — (dialec.) the plant producing the dark green pumpkin, and its fruit (cf. S. ḍaṅgarī, and ḍāṅgarī).
s.m. Mutual accommodation or assistance in tillage (consisting in the loan of oxen and ploughs without charge).
s.f. Partnership in cattle.
s.m. = dāhinā or dāhnā, q.v.
adv. (prov.), Skilfully, properly, well; thoroughly, perfectly; to the heart's content.
adj. (f. -inī), Giving, liberal, beneficent, bountiful, charitable.
adj. Low, mean, base, ignoble, vile; petty, paltry.
s.f. Receptacle, place, &c. (used in comp., e.g. tel-dānī).
s.f. Knowledge; science; learning

ety: v.n. of dānistan; rt. Zend dā, 'to know'
def: s.f. Knowledge; opinion.

ety: perf. part, of dānistan; see dānist
def: part. Known; knowing, having known; — adv. Knowingly, wittingly (generally in comp. with dīda, e.g. dīda o dānista).
s.f. Knowledge, &c. See dānish.
adj. Due, bestowable, worthy or fit to be given; worthy of having (anything) given.

ety: for A. دنية, rt. دني 'to be weak or contemptible'
def: s.f. A low or base quality, baseness meanness; vice, defect, blemish, imperfection.

r: dānīza, dānīzha, dāna, īza, īća, ća
ety: in P. also dānīzha; = dāna+dim. aff. īza = īća = ća
def: s.m. A lentil.
s.f. The post or mail, &c. ( = ḍāk, q.v.).
s.m. Foil (of a gem), &c. ( = ḍāk, q.v.).
s.m. = ḍank, q.v.
s.m. Sting (of a wasp, or scorpion, &c.); — nib (of a pen)

def: s.m. = ḍākā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. दण्डकः; but cf. ढक्का
def: s.m. A drum-stick (esp. the bass beating stick of the tabor); — a large kettle-drum; the bass end of a small kettle-drum; — (fig.) publicity, notoriety, fame (but only in combination with bajnā)
subs: (-kā), To beat or sound a drum; to exercise sway or authority, to rule, reign; to rejoice, make merry; to make (oneself, -apnā) famous (e.g. apnā ḍaṅkā bajānā); — to make (a person, or matter) notorious, to proclaim, make public, expose (e.g. uskā ḍaṅkā bajānā)
(-kā), To be famous, be notorious, be proclaimed
v.t. To proclaim by beat of drum, to make public, to proclaim aloud, to speak out.

def: s.f. Wages of a beater of the kettle-drum.
v.n. = ḍakārnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ḍākī, q.v.
adj. = ḍaṅkīlā, q.v.
adj. Possessed of a sting, stinging; — s.m. An animal that has a sting, stinger.
s.f. = ḍākinī, ḍāʼin, qq.v.

ety: ḍankī ( = ḍank)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To sting (as a reptile = ḍank mārnā, q.v.s.v. ḍaṅk).
v.n. = ḍāknā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍākū, q.v.
s.m. A stick (used in playing geṛī, q.v.); — membrum virile; — adj. (local), Large, great, enormous.
s.m. Liberality, bountifulness, beneficence.
s.m. = ḍānḍ, q.v.
s.m. = ḍānḍā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍānḍ, q.v.

s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Biting; stinging; a bite; sting; a tooth; armour, a coat of mail
subs: s.m. lit. 'Having a pungent root'; the plant Hyperanthera moringa, or a sort of horse-radish.

def: s.m. Sting (of a reptile); — a gnat, a large musquito; a gad-fly.
s.m. An animal that bites or stings, a biter; a dog; a snake; a gad-fly.
s.m. The act of biting, or stinging.
adj. Biting, mordacious; noxious, mischievous; cruel.
s.m. A large tooth, tusk, fang.
s.m. A biter; a stinger; any animal with tusks or large teeth; a boar; a hyena; a snake.
s.f. A small gad-fly.
part. Bitten; stung; — protected by mail or armour, mailed.
v.t. = ḍasnā, and ḍāsnā, qq.v.
v.t. = ḍasnā, q.v.
s.m. Tooth; tusk (of an elephant, or a boar)
subs: To bite the fore-finger in astonishment, or amazement, or perplexity, &c.; to indicate surprise, &c.
v.n. The teeth to chatter; — to squabble, wrangle; — chattering of the teeth; squabbling, &c.
To show the teeth, &c. ( = dāṅt nikālnā or nikosnā, qq.v.)
v.n. The teeth to be set or clenched (as in lockjaw, &c.)
v.t. To detract from the virtues or praise of (another)
v.n. The teeth to chatter, &c. ( = dāṅt bājnā)
To gnash the teeth (in anger, &c.); to grin
dāṅt-tale uṅglī dabānā or kāṭnā = dāṅt uṅglī kāṭnā
and dāṅtoṅ-meṅ uṅglī denā; — dāṅt tinkā, The taking a straw or a piece of grass in the mouth to express submission, or to deprecate anger (see dāṅtoṅ-meṅ tinkā lenā)
(-ke), To break the teeth (of); to render powerless, to humble, to bring (one) low (cf. Eng. 'to draw one's teeth')
(-ke), 'To knock out the teeth (of)'; to make one laugh the wrong side of his mouth
v.n. The teeth to fall out, or to be broken
(-par), To desire exceedingly, to covet (a thing); to be bent (on a thing, as revenge, &c.)
(-kī), 'To eat bread bitten by another'; to be an intimate friend (of), be in close friendship (with)
or kaćkaćānā, or kićkićānā, To grind the teeth, to gnash the teeth (in rage, &c.) = dāṅt pīsnā
(-ke), To set the teeth on edge; to excite the envy or jealousy (of); to dishearten, discourage; to displease; to baffle, to disappoint, to frustrate
(-ke), The teeth to be set on edge; to have (one's) cupidity, or envy, &c. excited; to be displeased, &c.
s.f. A preparation of wheat, poppy-seed, and sugar (a ceremony observed by Mohammadans on the appearance of a child's first tooth)
and dāṅt lānā, v.n. = dāṅt nikalnā, q.v.
(-par), To fix or set the teeth (upon)
v.n. The teeth to be set or clenched (as in lockjaw = dāṅt baiṭhnā)
(-par), To bite; — (met.) to acquire or get possession (of a thing), to possess (oneself) of
dāṅt nikāsnā, or nikosnā, or nikālnā, To show the teeth, to grin, to laugh; to express or confess inability or helplessness, to whine, to cringe
dāṅt nikalnā, v.n. To teeth; to grow (as teeth)
(-par), The teeth to be fixed (on); to desire eagerly, &c. ( = dāṅt rakhnā, q.v.)
(-ke), 'To have no tartar on the teeth' (through fasting); to be starving, be in poverty or destitution
v.n. To be cutting the teeth, be teething
(-ke), 'To come between the teeth (of)'; to provoke the envy or hatred (of)
'To seize the ground with the teeth'; to be reduced to extremities; to be in great pain
To be utterly powerless to keep (anything) in (one's) grasp
To gnash the teeth ( = dāṅt pīsnā)
To bite the fore-finger in indication of astonishment, or perplexity, or shame, or remorse, or sorrow, &c.; to stand amazed, be astonished, to wonder; to feel remorse, &c.
'To take a straw or a piece of grass in the mouth'; to express submission (to another), or to deprecate the anger of (the action implying, 'I am your cow, and therefore entitled to your protection')
(or zabān) honā, 'To be with the tongue between the teeth'; to live unmolested or uninjured in the midst of enemies
'The milk-teeth to be shed'; to be no longer a child, to have gained wisdom and experience (e.g. dūdh-ke dāṅt abhī nahīṅ ṭūṭe, 'he is yet unwise and inexperienced').
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دانت (dā̃t /⁠dānt⁠/) / داند (dā̃d /⁠dānd⁠/) / دند (/⁠dand⁠/) / دنت (/⁠dant⁠/), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀁𑀢 (daṃta), from Sanskrit दन्त (dánta), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hdántas, ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *h₃dónt-o-s, thematized from *h₃dónts (“tooth”). Doublet of دَنْدَان (dandān).

def: s.f. Rebuke; threat, &c. ( = ḍāṭ, q.v.)
s.f. = ḍāt̤; and ḍāṅṭh, ḍaṅṭhī, qq.v.
s.m. A large tooth; tooth (of a comb or saw); cog (of a wheel); dent, notch, jag
subs: (-meṅ), To be or become notched or jagged.

def: s.m. Tooth; tusk (of an elephant or boar)
subs: (˚ta+ar˚), s.m. Disease of the teeth; ulceration of the gums; gum-boil
s.m. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum pubescens (the petals of which resemble teeth in colour and shape)
s.m. The plant Strychnos potatorum (the flower of which is compared to a tooth)
s.m. 'Covering of the teeth'; the lip
s.m. Cleansing or washing the teeth; a tooth-brush or fibrous stick used for cleaning the teeth; — the tree Acacia catechu, &c. (the wood of which is used for cleaning the teeth)
s.m. A dentrifice composed chiefly of the powdered fruit of the myrobalan and green sulphate of iron
s.m. lit. 'Bad for the teeth'; — name of several trees with acid fruits; the common lime, Citrus acida; the elephant or wood-apple, Feronia elephantum; the plant Averrhoa carambola (acida), &c.
s.f. Proof, or attempted proof, by mere word of mouth (as distinguished from literary quotation); tradition; a statement without foundation, a fiction
The tartar of the teeth, any impurity of the teeth.

ety: prob. contrac. fr. dāṅtwan = datwan, q.v.
def: s.m. A piece of wood, or a twig (com. of the nīm, or the babūl tree), used as a tooth-brush; — (in Braj) pl. of dāṅt, q.v.
subs: To brush the teeth, and rinse the mouth.

def: s.f. A rake, a harrow.

ety: Prk. दंतइल्लो, and दंतइल्लओ; S. दन्त+इलः (and इल+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Having teeth, toothed; having large or projecting teeth, tusked; serrated, notched, jagged; (in Bot.) erose, sinuate; — an elephant; a boar
subs: s.m. A toothed or cogged wheel.

def: adj. & s.m. Having teeth, toothed; having large or projecting teeth, tusked; serrated, notched, jagged; (in Bot.) erose, sinuate; — an elephant; a boar
subs: s.m. A toothed or cogged wheel.

def: = H ڐانٿهل डांठल ḍāṅṭhal, s.m. [S. दण्ड+लः], = H ڐانٿهي डांठी ḍāṅṭhī, s.f. [S. दण्ड+इका], Pedicle, petiole, foot-stalk, stem, stalk, culm; straw, stubble, refuse of a harvest-floor; a knot in a stalk (of grain, &c.).

def: s.m. Stem, culm, stalk, pedicle, petiole, &c. ( = ḍānṭhī).
